title: Ask a Coast Guard Lawyer — Demo Question Set
description: 15 curated questions for presenting the CG Legal RAG system. Covers all 5 ingested documents. Mix of retrieval-only and LLM-summary modes. Questions selected for high retrieval confidence and clear, useful outputs.

questions.question: What are the three ways to commit an offense under Article 92?
questions.why: Article filter guarantees correct chunks. Three offenses clearly enumerated in text. Fast, visually clean output.

questions.question: What is the maximum punishment for larceny under Article 121?
questions.why: Punishment tables are structured — LLM synthesizes them into plain English clearly. Strong grounded output example.

questions.question: What are the elements required to prove desertion under Article 85?
questions.why: Legal elements lists are ideal for LLM synthesis. Shows the system reasoning about intent requirements.

questions.question: Under Article 112a, what does 'wrongful use' of a controlled substance mean?
questions.why: Demonstrates sub-article detection (112a). Verbatim definition text is more legally reliable than synthesis.

questions.question: What is the Coast Guard's policy on hazing and what does it prohibit?
questions.why: High-relevance topic for CGA cadets. Query expansion (hazing→Art.93) pulls cross-corpus content. LLM produces useful policy summary.

questions.question: What actions is a member required to take if they are arrested by civilian authorities?
questions.why: Precise procedural answer in CM 1.B.3.b. Shows non-MCM document retrieval. Verbatim requirement language is important here.

questions.question: What fraternization relationships are prohibited under the Conduct Manual?
questions.why: Complex multi-section policy with officer/enlisted nuance. LLM synthesizes clearly. Highly relevant to CGA cadet audience.

questions.question: What types of administrative investigations does the Coast Guard use and when is each required?
questions.why: Multi-section taxonomy (standard, formal board, LOD). LLM synthesizes the categories well. Shows AIM coverage.

questions.question: What rights does a party have during a Coast Guard administrative investigation?
questions.why: Ch10 F. chunks are directly responsive and well-structured. Verbatim rights language more reliable than synthesis.

questions.question: Under what conditions can an enlisted member be separated for misconduct involving drugs?
questions.why: High-confidence retrieval on Q. section. Multiple conditions that LLM structures clearly. Operationally relevant.

questions.question: What is the High Year Tenure policy and when does it require separation?
questions.why: Concrete policy with specific grade/year thresholds. Retrieval-only shows raw numbers clearly. Good numerical policy example.

questions.question: What happens to a Coast Guard member's career after a confirmed drug incident finding?
questions.why: Multi-step career consequence sequence — DI finding, separation initiation, characterization. LLM synthesizes the flow clearly.

questions.question: What are the separation consequences for a second alcohol incident?
questions.why: Demonstrates the reranker fix (previously a miss, now rank #1 with CE score 2.77). Section H content is specific and direct.

questions.question: Can a Coast Guard member face both a court-martial and administrative separation for the same drug violation?
questions.document: MCM 2024 + CG Military Substance Abuse Manual
questions.why: Cross-document synthesis from MCM Art.112a AND SA Manual. Shows multi-source capability. High practical relevance.

questions.question: What is an Article 31(b) rights warning and when must it be given during an investigation?
questions.document: CG Administrative Investigations Manual + CG Military Substance Abuse Manual
questions.why: Cross-document: AIM covers Art.31(b) in investigation context, SA Manual in DI context. Important concept for any CG operator.
